1.description: Expected out-of-sampleR2$\bm {R^{2}}$and norm of least-squares coefficient. 7.description: Out-of-sample market timing performance(T=12$\bm {T=12}$). This figure shows the out-of-sample prediction accuracy and portfolio performance estimates for the empirical analysis described in SectionV.C. The training window isT=12$T=12$months and RFF countP(orcT$cT$) ranges from 2 to 12,000 withγ=2$\gamma =2$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Out-of-sample market timing performance(T=12$\bm {T=12}$). This figure shows the out-of-sample prediction accuracy and portfolio performance estimates for the empirical analysis described in SectionV.C. The training window isT=12$T=12$months and RFF countP(orcT$cT$) ranges from 2 to 12,000 withγ=2$\gamma =2$. Alphas are versus a static position in the volatility-standardized market portfolio. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Out-of-sample market timing performance(T=60,120$\bm {T=60,120}$). This figure shows the out-of-sample prediction accuracy and portfolio performance estimates for the empirical analysis described in SectionV.C. The training window isT=60$T=60$or 120 months and RFF countP(orcT$cT$) ranges from 2 to 12,000 withγ=2$\gamma =2$. Alphas are versus a static position in the volatility-standardized market portfolio. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

10.description: Market timing positions. This figure shows the out-of-sample prediction accuracy and portfolio performance estimates for the empirical analysis described in SectionV.C. The training window isT=12$T=12$, 60, or 120 months withP=12,000$P=\text{12,000}$,z=103$z=10^{3}$, andγ=2$\gamma =2$. Positions are averaged across 1,000 sets of random feature weights. Plots show the six-month moving average of positions to improve readability. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

11.description: Variable importance. This figure shows the variable importance (VI) for theithpredictor that is the change in performance, defined as out-of-sampleR2or Sharpe ratio, moving from the full model with 15 variables to the reestimated model using 14 variables (excluding variablei). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
